{{nihongo|'''Kumi no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|久味国造|''kumi no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''kumi kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled the central portion of [[Iyo Province]] under the later {{ill|ritsuryō provinces|ja|令制国}}, corresponding to the central part of modern [[Ehime Prefecture]].

According to the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}, during the reign of [[Emperor Ōjin]], Iyonushi-no-Mikoto, the 13th-generation descendant of Kamimusubi-no-Mikoto, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko.

The Kumi no Kuni no Miyatsuko administered the southern portion of the later {{ill|Kume District, Ehime|ja|久米郡 (愛媛県)|lt=Kume District}}—that is, of [[Onsen District]]—and its [[descendants]] settled in the region and flourished as the {{ill|Kume clan|ja|久米氏}}. The Kume District established under the {{ill|ritsuryō|ja|律令制}} system is presumed to coincide with this kuni no miyatsuko's territory, and the position of {{ill|district magistrate|ja|郡司|lt=gunji}} was given to the Kume-no-Atai.

== References ==
* Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten Hensan Iinkai (ed.), ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten (Ehime-ken)'', Kadokawa Shoten, 1991, ISBN 4-04-001390-5, pp. 26, 273.

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:くみのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:伊予国]]
[[Category:愛媛県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:久味国造]]
